pubmed_links: [Gataulin et al. (2023) SARAF alters murine metabolic state via hypothalamic pathways, modulating HPA axis and anxiety](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37007714/); [Taha et al. (2025) Progressive elevation of SARAF and calcium pathway dysregulation in multiple sclerosis](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40429665/); [Palty et al. (2012) SARAF inactivates the store-operated calcium entry machinery to prevent excess calcium refilling](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22464749/)
summary: SARAF (store-operated calcium entry-associated regulatory factor) is an ER-resident protein that negatively regulates SOCE; whole-body knockout mice exhibit reduced hippocampal proliferation, altered HPA axis activity, and anxiety-related behavioral changes, implicating SARAF in stress and mood-relevant circuitry. Elevated SARAF levels have also been reported in multiple sclerosis patients, pointing to broader roles in neurological disease. (emerging evidence)
